title: KING COUNTY DIRECTORS ASSOCIATION – Disbursements, Employee Expenses, and Payroll/HR Records 2022-2026

communications.communication:
KING COUNTY DIRECTORS ASSOCIATION – Disbursements, Employee Expenses, and Payroll/HR Records 2022-2026

To the KCDA Public Records Officer:

Pursuant to the Washington State Public Records Act (RCW 42.56), I am requesting copies of the following identifiable public records that were created, received, or maintained in the care, custody, or control of the King County Directors' Association (KCDA) between January 1, 2022, and the present.

Please produce the complete, unredacted records for the following categories. Where applicable, this request specifically targets the records of KCDA Executive Director David Malhalko, Yohan Lee, Kristen Deskin, and any KCDA Board Members, but applies broadly to all KCDA expenditures as defined below:

1. Disbursements and Employee Expenses

 Credit Card (P-Card) Records: All itemized monthly account statements for all KCDA-issued credit cards, purchasing cards (P-Cards), or corporate charge accounts. This must include all individual corresponding receipts, point-of-sale invoices, and internal authorization/approval signatures for each charge.
 Travel and Expense Vouchers: All employee and executive travel pre-authorization forms, post-travel expense reports, mileage tracking logs, hotel folios, airline booking receipts, conference registration receipts, and per diem disbursement records.
 Invoices and Check Registers: The complete internal check register (or digital equivalent) detailing all checks cut or EFTs/wire transfers issued for employee reimbursements. Please include the underlying invoices, expense claims, or proof of purchase that justified each disbursement.

2. Executive Compensation and Bonus Structures

 Executive Contracts: The complete, fully executed employment contracts and subsequent amendments for KCDA executive staff (specifically including, but not limited to, David Malhalko and Yohan Lee).
 Bonus and Incentive Documentation: All records, memos, rubrics, and formulas detailing any performance bonuses, incentive pay, or variable compensation tied to KCDA’s collection of vendor sales, rebates, or administrative fees.
 Board Authorizations: All official Board of Directors resolutions, meeting minutes, or executive session summaries that formally approved, authorized, or altered the compensation, raises, or bonuses of KCDA executive staff.

3. Payroll and Human Resources

 Payroll Registers: Comprehensive payroll registers or master payroll summaries for KCDA executive and administrative staff, detailing gross pay, net pay, bonuses, stipends, benefits contributions, and deductions per pay period.
 Timesheets and Leave Records: All formal timesheets, clock-in/clock-out logs, or digital attendance records providing proof of hours actually worked by KCDA employees. Furthermore, please produce all tracking records for accrued, used, and cashed-out vacation, sick, or administrative leave.

RECORDS INSTALLMENTS

If production of these email records will be completed in multiple installments, please begin by producing responsive email records in REVERSE chronological order by date, producing the most recent messages and records first.

SEARCH FOR RESPONSIVE RECORDS

Your agency’s search for responsive email records should involve a good-faith search of all public employee personal devices reasonably believed to contain requested responsive records regardless the type of device or account from which the communication was generated or where the device or data is stored (including personal devices such as personal cell phones, laptops, tablets, and/or computers etc. used from home to conduct public business during the COVID-19 Pandemic). Nissen v. Pierce County, 183 Wa.2d 863 (2015).

Also, please provide a reasonably detailed, nonconclusory affidavit attesting to the nature and extent of any search of any privately owned public employee computer, device, or account.

SCOPE AND FORMAT

This request includes documents maintained natively on KCDA servers, physical HR/Accounting files, and data hosted within third-party financial or payroll software (e.g., ADP, QuickBooks, ERP systems) utilized by KCDA. Please provide these records in their native electronic format (such as Excel .xlsx files for ledgers) or as searchable PDFs.

If you claim any portion of these financial or personnel records is exempt from disclosure (such as specific bank routing numbers or personal social security numbers), you must redact only the specific exempt information and produce the remainder of the document. You must also provide a detailed exemption log citing the specific statutory basis for every redaction, as required by RCW 42.56.210(3).

EXEMPTION LOGS

Please ensure any agency redactions or exemptions claimed in the production of responsive records are accompanied by a complete and detailed exemption log noting the valid legal reason for each exemption at each redaction location in the record as well as the specific number of pages if any that your agency redacts or withholds in their entirety.

FEE ESTIMATE AND CONSOLIDATION

Pursuant to the Washington State Public Records Act mandate that agencies provide the 'fullest assistance to inquirers' (RCW 42.56.100) and utilize the 'most reasonable cost-efficient method available' for electronic records (RCW 42.56.120), KCDA must consolidate all responsive electronic records prior to generating any fee estimate. KCDA is expressly instructed not to artificially inflate production costs by 'unpacking,' extracting, or converting native bulk files (such as .pst email databases) into thousands of individual .pdf or .msg files in an attempt to exploit the statutory per-page or per-file fee structures. If individual electronic files or attachments are gathered, I require that KCDA consolidate them into a single compressed .zip file or a single PDF Portfolio *before* calculating the cost of production. Generating a fee estimate based on an artificially expanded file count or unnecessary format conversion—rather than utilizing the highly cost-efficient 10-cents-per-gigabyte native transmission rate or a consolidated single-file rate—constitutes an unreasonable barrier to public access and a direct violation of KCDA’s statutory duties.

PRIOR DESTRUCTION OF RECORDS

If any potentially relevant records have been destroyed, please provide copies of the destruction or transfer slips as well as any other documentation pertaining to, mentioning, or describing said transfer or destruction, including, but not limited to, a statement that your agency has no additional copies of the records in question.
Please ensure any agency redactions or exemptions claimed in the production of responsive records are accompanied by a complete and detailed exemption log noting the valid legal reason for each exemption at each redaction location in the record as well as the specific number of pages if any that your agency redacts or withholds in their entirety.

This request is not commercial in nature and does not seek a list of any kind in any or in any form.

This request is not for profit.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Thank you.
